notes: 32-byte (256-bit) output, default mode. Hex values are the 32-byte canonical hash output in lowercase. Vectors are pinned against the vendored BLAKE3 v1.8.5 output and will be cross-verified against the upstream test_vectors.json file in a follow-up pass.

vectors.name: empty input
vectors.notes: Well-known BLAKE3 empty-input constant; cited in upstream README and many third-party references.

vectors.name: 64-byte mod-251 pattern (single BLAKE3 block)
vectors.input_pattern: byte[i] = i mod 251 for i in 0..63
vectors.notes: Exactly one BLAKE3 block (BLAKE3_BLOCK_LEN = 64). Pattern matches the upstream test_vectors.json input convention but the output is pinned against the vendored v1.8.5 build until cross-verified against the upstream pack.

derive_key_vectors.name: ants-v1-test context, empty key_material
derive_key_vectors.output_hex: TBD — pin once ants-test-vectors regen-from-upstream lands; ants-client currently only asserts the output is non-zero
derive_key_vectors.notes: Placeholder; the actual pinned output will be generated alongside the BLAKE3 upstream test_vectors.json port.

streaming_property: The incremental API (init -> update* -> final) must produce byte-identical output to the one-shot ants_blake3_hash for any input. Tested in ants-client/foundation/crypto/tests/test_crypto.c by feeding a 100-byte input one byte at a time and comparing against the one-shot result.
